task_completion.description: Construct at least 4 partition wall segments to divide the space into functional zones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for at least 4 new interior wall segments drawn in the 2D plan view of Sweet Home 3D, dividing the open shell into distinct rooms.

task_completion.description: Define rooms and apply distinct floor textures or colors to at least 3 zones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for room areas defined in the 2D plan (often indicated by room name labels and square footage) with at least 3 distinct floor colors or textures visible in the 2D plan or the bottom 3D view.

task_completion.description: Furnish the Reception Area (at least 1 desk/counter, 1 office chair, 2 lounge items) and Locker Rooms (at least 4 lockers, 2 toilets, 2 sinks, 2 showers).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for these specific furniture items placed in their respective zones in the 2D plan or 3D view. The furniture list panel on the left side of the screen should also show these items added to the project.

task_completion.description: Furnish the Main Gym Floor with at least 8 pieces of gym equipment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for at least 8 pieces of fitness/gym equipment (e.g., treadmills, exercise bikes, weight benches, ellipticals) placed in the main gym area in the 2D plan or 3D view.

task_completion.description: Export a 3D render of the interior to ~/Desktop/gym_render.png.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file named 'gym_render.png' appearing on the Desktop, an image viewer showing the rendered interior, or the 'Create photo' dialog in Sweet Home 3D successfully saving the image to the Desktop.

task_completion.description: Save the Sweet Home 3D project file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Sweet Home 3D window showing the project is saved (the asterisk '*' next to the filename 'fitness_center_starter.sh3d' in the title bar should disappear), or a terminal/file manager showing the file was recently modified.

integrity.description: The agent used the Sweet Home 3D graphical interface to design the layout.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Sweet Home 3D application open with active manipulation of walls, rooms, and furniture, rather than just terminal commands editing XML/files.

integrity.description: The 3D render was generated using Sweet Home 3D's photo rendering tool.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Create photo' dialog box in Sweet Home 3D or a progress bar indicating rendering, rather than a downloaded image from the internet or a simple screenshot of the UI being renamed.

integrity.description: The agent built the design from the starter shell rather than downloading or substituting a completed file.
integrity.visual_evidence: A progression of screenshots showing the empty shell gradually being filled with walls, room definitions, and furniture.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires modifying 'fitness_center_starter.sh3d' (which starts as a blank structural shell). The maximum score is 100 points, distributed across 5 main criteria: C1 (20 pts) for >=4 partition wall segments; C2 (15 pts) for >=3 zones with distinct floor textures; C3 (25 pts) for specific furniture placement (Reception: 1 desk, 1 office chair, 2 lounge items; Locker Rooms: 4 lockers, 2 toilets, 2 sinks, 2 showers; Main Gym: 8 gym equipment); C4 (20 pts) for exporting 'gym_render.png' to the Desktop; C5 (20 pts) for saving the project file.
